name: VOD2MLIB
description: Generate .strm files for Dispatcharr VOD movies and series, with optional NFO metadata, batch processing, and scheduled auto-rescan.
author: R3XCHRIS

fields.label: Root Folder for Movies
fields.help_text: Path inside the Dispatcharr container where movie folders will be created. Map a host folder to /VODS in your container.

fields.label: Root Folder for Series
fields.help_text: Path inside the Dispatcharr container where series folders will be created.

fields.label: Dispatcharr URL
fields.help_text: Full URL to your Dispatcharr instance (NOT localhost). Baked into .strm files, so it must be reachable from your media server.

fields.label: Batch Size (Movies)
fields.help_text: Number of movies to process in this run. Start small (10) to verify, then scale up.

fields.label: Generate Movie NFO Files
fields.help_text: Create .nfo metadata files alongside each movie .strm.

fields.label: Batch Size (Series)
fields.help_text: Number of series to process. Episodes are auto-fetched per series.

fields.label: Generate Series NFO Files
fields.help_text: Create tvshow.nfo and per-episode .nfo metadata files.

fields.label: Auto-Rescan Schedule (cron)
fields.help_text: Standard 5-field cron: 'minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week'. Default '0 3 * * *' = every day at 03:00. Used by Apply Schedule.

fields.label: Scheduled Action
fields.help_text: Which action the scheduler should run on each tick.

actions.label: Scan for VODs to Convert
actions.description: Show total movies and series available in Dispatcharr.

actions.label: Generate Movie .strm Files
actions.description: Process movies according to batch size.

actions.label: Generate Series .strm Files
actions.description: Fetch episodes + create .strm files (auto-fetch per series, 3 parallel workers).

actions.label: Rescan All (Movies + Series)
actions.description: One-shot full rescan: scan totals, then generate movies, then series. This is what the cron schedule calls.

actions.label: Apply Schedule
actions.description: Register or update the periodic auto-rescan task using the cron expression in settings. Snapshots current settings into the task.

actions.label: Show Schedule Status
actions.description: Show whether a scheduled rescan is registered, its cron expression, last run, and run count.

confirm.title: Remove auto-rescan schedule?
confirm.message: This unregisters the periodic task. You can re-create it any time with Apply Schedule.

actions.label: Clean Up Movies
actions.description: Remove plugin-generated .strm and .nfo files from the movies root. Empty folders are removed; folders with user-added files (subtitles, posters) are preserved.

confirm.title: Delete all generated movie files?
confirm.message: This deletes every .strm and .nfo file under your Movies root that this plugin created. User-added files in those folders are preserved. Continue?

actions.label: Clean Up Series
actions.description: Remove plugin-generated .strm and .nfo files from the series root. Empty Season/series folders are removed; folders with user-added files are preserved.

confirm.title: Delete all generated series files?
confirm.message: This deletes every .strm and .nfo file under your Series root that this plugin created. User-added files in those folders are preserved. Continue?
